mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is at or near the effective lower bound, negative surprises in inflation support an equity rally because real policy rates fall and discount rates stay compressed. Lower CPIAUCSL growth should therefore map to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:federal funds rate near zero in mid-2009
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL declines while FEDFUNDS is below 0.50% but SPX forward 20-day returns are negative in a majority of triggered cases, or the triggered group underperforms the unconditional baseline, reject this mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate reflects deteriorating household income and weaker final demand, which should reduce equity earnings expectations and push S&P 500 returns negative over the next month. The signal is strongest when UNRATE is already elevated and continues to rise.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises by over 0.3 percentage points in 3 months, or if the conditional average return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in INDPRO after a recessionary trough signals stabilization in industrial activity and inventory liquidation ending, which should support cyclical equity valuations. Rising INDPRO over the prior quarter should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production remained depressed in June 2009
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month changes in INDPRO do not correspond to positive average 60-day SPX forward returns, or if the sign of the relation is reversed in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strength in RSAFS indicates improving nominal consumer and business spending, which supports revenue expectations and operating leverage for large-cap equities. A positive month-over-month change in RSAFS should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:retail and food services sales were depressed in mid-2009
failure_condition: Reject if rising RSAFS fails to improve the hit rate of positive 20-day SPX returns above 50% or produces a negative conditional average return across rolling out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts act as a leading demand indicator for construction, durable goods, and bank credit. When HOUST rises sharply from a low base, equity markets should reprice toward recovery and produce positive forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST increases by at least 10% from the prior month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was still near recessionary lows in May 2009
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month HOUST increases above 10% are followed by negative SPX 20-day returns more often than positive returns, or if the signal is not distinguishable from noise in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in GDP coincident with higher retail sales indicates broad-based demand recovery that should lift earnings expectations and reduce recession risk premiums. Positive quarterly GDPC1 growth combined with rising RSAFS should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available GDP vintage was weak in early 2009
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of positive GDPC1 and rising RSAFS does not outperform either series alone in predicting 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal yields no positive edge in rolling tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market stabilization regime, falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE jointly signal worsening payroll conditions that depress consumer spending and earnings expectations, making near-term equity returns negative. The effect should be strongest when both indicators worsen together over the prior month.
evidence_refs: fact:payrolls were still falling in June 2009
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases do not produce negative 5-day SPX forward returns more often than not, or if the interaction term is not significant in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: During a tightening cycle, higher FEDFUNDS should compress valuation multiples and weaken equity returns, especially when inflation is also accelerating. A rising policy rate combined with an uptick in CPIAUCSL should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 25 bps within 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if rate-hike episodes with rising CPIAUCSL do not correspond to below-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign of the conditional effect becomes positive across rolling out-of-sample windows.
